compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan-review PASSED (all advisory, no BLOCK; attestation signed @6a28e9a5a). Advisory triage: The T8/E2 findings ('WS1 never names the sub-answers', 'WS3 never names criterion ids/exec/applies_at', 'WS9 cohort field type/schema', 'WS1-10 are compound labels needing an external doc') all concern the EPIC being a thin coordination/index ticket — by design, per the guiding principle 'criterion-specific logic lives in child rubrics/tickets, not the coordinating parent'. Each detail the findings ask for is fully specified in the corresponding CHILD story (verified: every child carries file_impact + typed ACs + baked design decisions from session brief-bride-rash). Genuinely-actionable coaching is folded into child implementation, not the epic text (editing the epic now would stale the fresh attestation): (1) WS9 — record cohort backward-compat policy (missing=unknown) as a docs schema note [already in WS9 AC]; (2) WS5 — add a recall/precision eval case per rubric enrichment (T3/G3/T5c/T11/E2/G4), not only E6; (3) WS3 — note the structural alternative (reuse existing overlay_routing:llm T10/T11 path vs a new value) in the ticket [already decided: reuse]; (4) WS4/WS5 lexicon ownership — deliberately in-prompt (rubric strings), the rebar architecture choice, not config-driven. AC2 (link design/placeholder epics) is already satisfied by the epic's 4 relates_to links (grumpy-gland-rose/only-crave-art/fort-wisp-wren/spin-cub-usher). AC3/SC1 'all 11 WS complete' is the multi-session epic-completion criterion, satisfied at close, not in one session.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: EPIC COMPLETE — all 11 child stories (WS1-WS11) created, implemented, and CLOSED with BOTH attestations (plan-review + completion-verifier), linked as parent_id children (rebar's containment hierarchy — NOT deps links, per CLAUDE.md 'parent work with parent_id, not a link relation'; the deps array is correctly relates_to-only + the 4 placeholder-epic links). Closed children: WS1 neat-spa-ulcer (4f20635dd, Pass-2 vocab), WS6 dork-quota-zeal (723f30f1c, self-calibration), WS7 snappy-yew-strafe (f0b736ce8, prompt-hardening), WS2 catty-noun-lyre (af2f4df01, E4+hedge), WS3 win-attic-wren (0c89397cc, T13/T14 overlays), WS11 pot-augur-ogle (0b73ea24e, removal-rationale), WS4 rife-feint-canal (47307af01, DET verify-lint), WS5 male-welt-inlet (277480d6f, rubric enrichments), WS8 orb-jet-folk (85aa654eb, MOVE_REGISTRY), WS9 sip-thorn-epoch (54fa128fa, cohort field), WS10 glad-gloom-bog (b4e70a551, docs+explain). All DSO-sourced (WS1-10) + the net-new Chesterton's Fence (WS11). Full local suite green; landing the 11-commit stack via Gerrit onto main.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

Adoption epic for the DSO vs rebar plan-review gap analysis (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md). Each remediation was evaluated in a design session (2026-07) against rebar's architecture (four-pass find->verify->decide->coach, deterministic Pass-3, locked coach MOVE_REGISTRY, DET floor, REVIEW_RESULT sidecar) under one lens: does rebar already solve this better at a different level, and does the remediation work WITH rebar's grain?

Guiding principle preserved throughout: rebar's multi-pass separation is the reliable substrate (DSO's own bug history validates it). We adopt DSO's CONTENT (detection lexicons, named FP classes, calibration idioms) to upgrade pass *content* to match pass *structure* -- never importing DSO's weaker mechanisms (self-scored persona committees, hard-gate anti-rationalization prose, lexical word-match).

Scope note: WS1-WS10 are DSO-report-sourced adoptions. WS11 (removal-rationale / Chesterton's Fence) is a NET-NEW criterion gap surfaced during this evaluation -- NOT in the DSO report -- filed here as an 11th workstream per design decision, so the epic is "plan-review criteria gaps found in this evaluation" (DSO-sourced + one net-new).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each of the 11 adopted workstreams (child stories WS1-WS11) is implemented and eval-guarded where it changes reviewer behavior. "Eval-guarded" = a change that alters reviewer OUTPUT (finder/verifier/coach behavior) ships with a recall AND/OR precision case in the eval-spec harness (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/*.eval.yaml); a change that does not alter reviewer output (docs, mechanical, sidecar plumbing) ships with a deterministic unit check instead.
- [ ] Generic passes (Pass 2 verify contract, Pass 4 coach) stay generic across findings -- criterion-specific logic lives in criterion rubrics/evidence, not in the shared passes (WS11's sub-answers are criterion-local, not GRADED_BINARY additions)
- [ ] No new blocking behavior ships without calibration evidence (advisory-by-default, consistent with existing gate posture)
- [ ] Prompt/routing CI drift-parity gate stays green after rubric edits

## Context (evaluated but NOT in this epic)
- G-1+G-2 (conflict/oscillation) -> epic grumpy-gland-rose (dedicated brainstorm)
- G-6 (cross-ticket interaction) -> epic only-crave-art (store-wide, outside the gate)
- R-1 part 2 (blocking-tier context isolation) -> placeholder epic fort-wisp-wren (depends on R-1 part 1 cohort field, shipped here)
- R-4 (calibration-example library) -> placeholder epic spin-cub-usher
- REJECTED: R-2 (double-verify; distinct criteria already give perspective diversity), T9 RETIRE-row rule (nit), the persona-committee / hard-gate-prose / word-match items (Section 7)
- NOTED, separate track: E5/E6 proxy-validation & cutover-e2e are advisory, so bypass-the-real-path incomplete implementations can slip both gates -- a threshold-calibration question handled outside this epic.

## Workstreams (child stories)
- WS1 Pass-2 vocabulary expansion; WS2 E4/exec:DET detectors; WS3 enumeration-overlay; WS4 DET verify-command blacklist; WS5 rubric enrichments; WS6 self-calibration anchors; WS7 prompt-hardening pass; WS8 coach moves; WS9 sidecar cohort field; WS10 docs + authoring surface (all DSO-sourced).
- WS11 removal-rationale criterion (Chesterton's Fence, removal-side dual of A1) -- NET-NEW, not from the DSO report.

## Integration ordering (shared files -- land in this order to avoid clobbering)
- plan_review_verifier.md + _agentic.md are edited by WS1 (new sub-answers), WS6 (blast_radius ratchet), WS7 (shared preamble). Land WS1 -> WS6 -> WS7.
- docs/plan-review-gate.md is edited by WS8 (MOVE_REGISTRY table), WS10 (R-3 invariant + tuning checklist) -- disjoint sections; coordinate the two edits.
- criteria_routing.json is edited by WS2 (hedge 1-TURN + E4), WS3 (two overlay criteria) -- disjoint entries.
- passes.py is edited by WS7 (_resolve_system preamble), WS8 (MOVE_REGISTRY), WS9 (cohort stamps) -- disjoint functions.
- WS9 (cohort field) is the prerequisite for placeholder epic fort-wisp-wren.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 11 child stories created capturing the design decisions (WS1-WS10 DSO-sourced + WS11 net-new Chesterton's Fence)
- [ ] Related design/placeholder epics linked (grumpy-gland-rose, only-crave-art, fort-wisp-wren, spin-cub-usher)
- [ ] This epic is the durable home for the plan-review criteria-gap effort surfaced in this evaluation
compiled_state.title: Adopt DSO plan-review gap findings (content upgrades to rebar's four-pass gate)
